evals.prompt: Evaluate this skill for me — is it worth shipping?
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges the form is fine, runs/reasons a baseline comparison, reports a near-zero measured delta, and leads with value.
expectations: Acknowledges the skill is well-formed and would trigger (form is fine)
Runs, or explicitly reasons through, a with-skill versus no-skill baseline comparison and reports a measured pass-rate delta rather than only asserting it is 'redundant'
Concludes the value delta is approximately zero, i.e. the skill is dead weight
Leads the verdict with measured value and does not declare the skill effective from reading alone

evals.expected_output: Flags the weak description, quantifies the body's value as a baseline delta, distinguishes loaded from realized value, and keeps the local rule.
expectations: Flags the description as first-person and vague, lacking both what it does and when to use it
Quantifies the body's value as a measured baseline delta (a no-skill run fails on the test-account exclusion / billing.invoices table where a with-skill run passes)
Distinguishes loaded value from realized value: notes the strong body is stranded behind a description that will not reliably trigger
Recommends a concrete third-person what+when rewrite while keeping the test-account exclusion as valuable local knowledge

evals.prompt: review the quality of this skill
evals.expected_output: Flags the general-knowledge padding as cut, keeps the local rule, frames with keep/cut/push, and notes form passes but value does not.
expectations: Flags the explanation of what a PDF is (and similar general knowledge) as content to cut
Identifies the cover-page rule (start extraction at page 2) as genuine local knowledge to keep
Frames its recommendations explicitly with the keep / cut / push distinction
Notes that a linter would pass this skill, so the bloat is a judgement issue beyond mechanical checks
